title: Dupatya reclinata
taxonomy: Dupatya reclinata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to tropical regions of Asia, and is often found in woodland and grassland habitats. This plant is a type of climber or twiner, with stems that can grow up to several meters in length. The classification of Dupatya reclinata is further detailed at the genus and species levels within the Apocynaceae family. Further research may be necessary to determine the specific classification of this species.
uses: Dupatya reclinata, also known as climbing milk bush, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help in treating wounds and infections. In traditional African medicine, it is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas and infusions, which are believed to have various health benefits. Additionally, its roots and leaves are also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and other ailments.
